title: Topological protection as a defect-tolerant reservoir primitive: a pre-registered simulation study of when it holds
description: A pre-registered simulation study of the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) resonator chain as a physical reservoir. Main finding, stated as a conditional: topological structure protects a reservoir's computation against a dead element exactly when chiral symmetry holds -- several-fold in the idealized tight-binding model on a linear task, weaker under nonlinearity (NARMA10) and noise, beaten by generic redundancy, and only marginally (statistically inconclusive) recovered in a physically faithful damped/Duffing model. A separate result: the trained readout cancels structured (low-rank) noise, leaving only the full-rank thermal floor. Includes the full preprint (preprint.pdf), an extended lab report with an honest ledger of retracted claims, ~20 pre-registered experiment scripts with a self-test, an interactive dashboard, and animations. Conducted under the gbranaa4-hue method: pre-register the test, validate the instrument, split the average, find the boundary, and write the 'no' as carefully as the 'yes'.
